{{Quote|Ota getting too cold... even for Snogars!|A Snogar, to Luke Skywalker and Boba Fett|<ref name="ota" />}}
'''Ota''' was a frozen world in the [[Kashyyyk Region]] of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] which was the home of the furry [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] known as the [[Snogar]]s. The Snogars lived in [[City/Legends|cities]] built within the planet's [[Water/Legends|ice]] canyons by their ancestors, a highly intelligent race referred to as the "Old Ones." By [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]] the heating machines that kept the Snogar cities warm began to fail, leading Ota's natives to seek out offworlders who could repair the devices and help them survive the planet's biting cold.

==Description==
Mostly hidden by the fringes of the [[Thornhedge Nebula/Legends|Thornhedge Nebula]], Ota was an [[Water/Legends|icy]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] in the [[Kashyyyk Region]]'s [[Mytaranor sector/Legends|Mytaranor sector]], sitting at the junction of the [[Dauntless Run]] and the [[Ootmian Pabol/Legends|''Ootmian Pabol'']].<ref name="TEA" /> A freezing world, Ota was characterized by massive ice canyons and crevasses big enough to fly a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] through.<ref name="ota" /> A [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] known as the [[Snogar]]s lived in shivering cities above ground, the ice contained hidden dangers, including massive serpent-like creatures that could emerge suddenly to attack travelers.<ref name="ota" />

==History==
{{Quote|Old ones make city... make power... many cycles past! But... old ones all die!|A Snogar, to Luke Skywalker and Boba Fett|<ref name="ota" />}}
[[File:OtaPursuit.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Luke Skywalker pursues Boba Fett through the ice canyons of Ota.]]
In the millennia before the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], a civilization of technologically advanced [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] later referred to as the "Old Ones" flourished on Ota, building massive [[City/Legends|cities]] among the planet's ice valleys. However, over time, the Old Ones degenerated into the less-intelligent Snogars, whose society devolved as they forgot how to use the technology that powered and heated their communities.<ref name="egas">[[The Essential Guide to Alien Species (real-world book)|''The Essential Guide to Alien Species'']]</ref> During the latter [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]], the wealthy [[Tefaun]] [[Banker/Legends|banker]] [[Kelek]] "the Blue" funded [[Mining/Legends|mining]] opportunities on Ota,<ref name="wheel">{{SW|url=news/viva-space-vegas-the-history-of-the-marvelous-wheel-part-1|series=Viva Space Vegas! The History of the Marvelous Wheel|text=Part 1|nolive=1}}</ref> but by the era of the Galactic Civil War the planet was a complete backwater, inhabited solely by the Snogars freezing in their ancient cities.<ref name="ota" />

By [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]] the Snogars had become desperate to find some way to fix the technology that kept their homes heated, and staked all their hopes on the arrival of offworlders—or, as they called them, "smart ones"—who had the technical knowledge necessary to save them. Their prayers were seemingly answered when [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] pursued [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] to Ota—Fett, on behalf of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperials]], was hunting a rogue Imperial [[Spy/Legends|spy]] believed to be hiding on the world. A group of Snogars from a nearby city captured the pair and ordered them to fix the [[Hydroelectric power generator|hydroelectric generator]] that powered its heating machine, and when the two offworlders escaped, they instead kidnapped Skywalker's friend [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], who had followed Skywalker to Ota alongside Rebel associates [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]]. Fett, also pursuing a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] on Solo, returned to the city to track him down, leading to a confrontation between the Rebels, Fett, and a group of angry Snogars wanting Fett's [[Blood/Legends|blood]].<ref name="ota" />

Fett chased the Snogars off, then found that the Imperial mole had taken refuge in a part of the city shunned by the natives. However, the Rebels overpowered the bounty hunter and trapped him using a massive [[Magnet/Legends|magnet]], allowing them to flee Ota with the defecting spy.<ref name="ota" />

==Inhabitants==
Ota was home to the Snogar species, a race of tall, furry humanoids of limited intelligence who lived within the cities built by their ancestors, the "Old Ones."<ref name="egas" /> Although the Snogars grew up on the freezing world and were adept in traversing its snowdrifts and ice canyons, the cold often grew too great even for them when they were away from the heating machines of their cities. Although offworlders were rare on Ota, the Snogars thus became dependent on their help to be able to survive.<ref name="ota" />

==Behind the scenes==
Ota first appeared as the setting of ''[[The Frozen World of Ota]]'', a [[Star Wars (comic strip)|comic strip]] story arc written by [[Russ Manning]], [[Rick Hoberg]] and [[Don Christensen]] and released in the summer of [[1980]]. As ''The Frozen World of Ota'' contains one of the earliest-ever appearances of Boba Fett,<ref name="ota" /> the planet was later mentioned in numerous later sources.<ref name="galaxy">{{GalaxyCite|1|Boba Fett: Mystery Man in Not-So-Shining Armor}}</ref><ref name="insider">{{InsiderCite|106|Classic Moment|Classic Moment: Boba Fett Encounter}}</ref>

==Appearances==
*"[[The Frozen World of Ota]]" {{Reprint|The Frozen World of Ota}} {{1st}}
